model_stance_conclusion_text: I disagree with the statement that we should abandon marriage.

nodes.node_text: 1. **Changing Social Norms**: Marriage is no longer the only socially acceptable way to form a committed relationship.

argument_list.matched_argument: as society and values change, marriage is no longer as popular as it once was and divorce rates continue to climb.
marriage is an expensive and outdated institution that has failed to keep up with changing societal norms.
marriage is an outdated institution that has little meaning in today's society when it is perfectly acceptable for unmarried couples to live together.
marriage is an outdated tradition that does not account for our changing society.
we should abandon marriage since now it doesn't seem as important as it used to be. it's easy to get divorced and now it's acceptable to have children out of wedlock or multiple partners.
with the divorce rates so high, marriage is pointless. and in recent times less and less people are even getting married. they just cohabitate and commit. marriage is becoming outdated.

nodes.node_text: Cohabitation, single parenthood, and other family structures are increasingly common and accepted.

argument_list.matched_argument: as society and values change, marriage is no longer as popular as it once was and divorce rates continue to climb.
marriage is an outdated institution that has little meaning in today's society when it is perfectly acceptable for unmarried couples to live together.
Marriage is an outdated institution. All types of relationships and family units (one-parent families, homosexual relationships, etc.) should be embraced and treated equally.
Marriage is becoming obsolete with more and more youth cohabiting without the need to celebrate anything. Celebration is costly and deprive couples the means for sustaining their family
Marriage is over rated and now many people just prefer to live together.
Marriage is rare these days, many couples choose to live together and not get married.
more and more couples are opting to not get married, and the institution is considered by many to be quite old fashioned.
too many marriages end up in divorce and many couples are just living together without it now.
we should abandon marriage since now it doesn't seem as important as it used to be. it's easy to get divorced and now it's acceptable to have children out of wedlock or multiple partners.
with the divorce rates so high, marriage is pointless. and in recent times less and less people are even getting married. they just cohabitate and commit. marriage is becoming outdated.

nodes.node_text: 2. **Individual Freedom**: Marriage can impose legal and societal expectations that limit personal freedom.

argument_list.matched_argument: marriage makes you lose your personal freedom and having to share everything with your partner, it should be abandoned
Marriage restricts freedom. A person can't do as he or she pleases without considering the needs and wishes of his/her spouse.
marriage should be abandoned because a person is able to have way more freedom
we should abandon marriage it restricts the freedom of an individual

nodes.node_text: Abandoning marriage could allow individuals to define their relationships on their own terms.

argument_list.matched_argument: marriage is antiquated. should be free to live in the kind of relationship based on their feelings and their commitment

nodes.node_text: 3. **High Divorce Rates**: The prevalence of divorce suggests that marriage may not be a sustainable institution for many people.

argument_list.matched_argument: as society and values change, marriage is no longer as popular as it once was and divorce rates continue to climb.
fifty percent of marriages end in divorce which is letting us know that the idea of marriage as lifelong  is no longer realistic
marriage is an outdated custom - so many people divorce that it seems pointless
Marriage is an outdated institution that rarely last very long anyway.
marriage is an outdated ritual that ends in divorce.
marriage is an outdated tradition that usually ends in divorce anyways.
marriage is no longer for life as is seen by the high divorce rates
marriage used to be sacred between a loving couple.   it seems so many people abuse their promises and divorce over and over again.   it's lost it's meaning and value.   we should simply abandon it.
so many marriages end in divorce these days that there barely seems any point in it.
the divorce rates continue to rise which shows that fewer people believe in a life long committment
We should abandon marriage as with divorce rates so high it is clear that is has become an outmoded concept.
we should abandon marriage because it's an outdated notion that in this day in age usually ends in divorce anyways.
with divorce rates up so high, we should abandon marriage as an archaic and broken tradition.
with the divorce rates so high, marriage is pointless. and in recent times less and less people are even getting married. they just cohabitate and commit. marriage is becoming outdated.

nodes.node_text: Abandoning it could reduce the stigma and emotional toll associated with failed marriages.

argument_list.matched_argument: Marriage is an outdated concept which cause emotional trauma when it doesn't work out and people have to dissolve it legally.

nodes.node_text: 4. **Gender Equality**: Historically, marriage has been tied to patriarchal structures.

argument_list.matched_argument: Marriage as a social institution is based on outdated notions of exchanging property and other resources in order to purchase or take ownership over a woman, and these practices should be abandoned.
marriage has roots in patriarchy and misogyny therefore it is time to move on to a new system.
marriage is a dated institution that unfairly benefits the patriarchy.
marriage is a social construct to oppress women & is backed by & enforced by the patriarchy
marriage is an institution founded on the outdated notion that women are property and should be discontinued
marriage is an out-dated patriarchal system of female ownership that has no place in a modern equal society.
marriage is an out-dated patriarchal system of ownership of women that has no place in modern societies.
Marriage reinforces traditional social norms of the woman in the household and the male being the provider, which both entrenches patriarchal and heteronormative ideas within our society.
some say that marriage is inherently misogynistic

nodes.node_text: Abandoning it could promote more egalitarian relationships and reduce gender-based inequalities.

argument_list.matched_argument: Marriage as a social institution is based on outdated notions of exchanging property and other resources in order to purchase or take ownership over a woman, and these practices should be abandoned.
marriage has roots in patriarchy and misogyny therefore it is time to move on to a new system.
marriage is a dated institution that unfairly benefits the patriarchy.
marriage is a social construct to oppress women & is backed by & enforced by the patriarchy
marriage is an out-dated patriarchal system of female ownership that has no place in a modern equal society.
marriage is an out-dated patriarchal system of ownership of women that has no place in modern societies.
Marriage reinforces traditional social norms of the woman in the household and the male being the provider, which both entrenches patriarchal and heteronormative ideas within our society.

nodes.node_text: 5. **Economic and Legal Burdens**: Marriage can complicate financial and legal matters, such as taxes, inheritance, and divorce proceedings.

argument_list.matched_argument: by adopting such practice we can get rid of all the burdens associate with marriage like financial, legal and social obligations.
marriage is an outdated concept that does more harm than good.  most relationships don't work out forever, and making a person tied to another legally and financially forever is not fair.

marriage is an outdated insitution that is not seen in the same way as it was years ago tying two people together and costs both parties a huge amount of money to get out of if they want to end the marriage.
marriage is antiquated and does not provide much benefits in regards to legalities and can even reult in higher taxes with joint filing.
marriage is nothing more than a piece of paper that creates financial problems.
marriage is out dated now as people who marry rarely stay together for life you are also responsible foryour spouse so could be responsible for their debts

marriage was started as a contract to support a wife and children.   in today's world many marriages fail causing a great cost to divorce,  it would be easier and less expensive not to have the contract at all.
since it is an outdated concept. modern unions consist of many different types of couples who are unlikely to be life long partners and marriage creates a costly legal nightmare when they breakup.
we should abandon marriage because it just causes people to have to spend huge amounts of money on attorney fees should they decide they do not want to live together any longer.
when a marriage breaks down it can cause a lot of stress and money to legally end it. it would be simpler if there was no marriage in place to start and both parties could just move on.

nodes.node_text: Abandoning it could simplify these issues.

argument_list.matched_argument: Abandoning marriage means no ugly divorce battle if things don't work out.
by adopting such practice we can get rid of all the burdens associate with marriage like financial, legal and social obligations.
marriage can be difficult to dissolve if the relationship breaks down. without marriage the couple will be able to get on with their separate lives much quicker.
marriage is nothing but a legal contract between two people, the process can be greatly simplified under a different and less contentious guise

marriage was started as a contract to support a wife and children.   in today's world many marriages fail causing a great cost to divorce,  it would be easier and less expensive not to have the contract at all.
people can live together and function without actually being married. makes things easier if they want to split up later.
there is no need for marriage in this society.  too many marriages end in divorce.  it would be easier to just get rid of that altogether.  safe money and stress.
too many marriages now are falling apart.  if marriage was abandoned then it would be much easier to separate and less need for legal interference.
when a marriage breaks down it can cause a lot of stress and money to legally end it. it would be simpler if there was no marriage in place to start and both parties could just move on.

nodes.node_text: 1. **Social Stability**: Marriage has traditionally provided a stable framework for raising children and fostering community ties.

argument_list.matched_argument: Marriage has been the foundation of the family unit for ages and abandoning it would contribute to the breakup of society.
marriage is one of the essential elements of society. it is centered around the concept of forming strong, coherent and dependable family.
marriage is the bedrock of society that allows for it's continuation and abandonment could cause the collapse of cohesion, normative agreement and morality for civilization.
marriage is the bedrock of stable family life in almost every community in the world and must not be banned.
marriage is the best basis of a stable family unit and should be supported and continued.
marriage is the foundation for our society's basic family structure
marriage provides stability and a commitment between people which strengthens relationships and to abandon it would be to turn out backs on something good.
marriage provides stability and it helps families
marriage provides stability for the individual and for the family.   happily married people are more productive in society

Married couples have become a foundational part of how we build our societies, raise our children, and pass down history and wealth, and abandoning marriage altogether would have a number of unforeseen impacts on our societies.
we should not abandon marriage because it is an important commitment that helps keep society going.
we shouldn't abandon marriage because is important for society and union for people

nodes.node_text: Abandoning it could lead to societal fragmentation.

argument_list.matched_argument: Marriage has been the foundation of the family unit for ages and abandoning it would contribute to the breakup of society.
marriage is the bedrock of society that allows for it's continuation and abandonment could cause the collapse of cohesion, normative agreement and morality for civilization.

Married couples have become a foundational part of how we build our societies, raise our children, and pass down history and wealth, and abandoning marriage altogether would have a number of unforeseen impacts on our societies.

nodes.node_text: 2. **Legal Protections**: Marriage offers legal benefits, such as spousal rights, inheritance, and healthcare access, that are not easily replicated in other relationship structures.

argument_list.matched_argument: marriage allows the legality of a spouse to be able to obtain medical information in the instance that a spouse is sick and/or hospitalized as well as acces to healthcare under a spouse's coverage plan.
marriage can show commitment and values it can give you rights and privileges under social security law and tax benefits
marriage contributes a solild framework and structure to a relationship, gives legal representation and stability.
marriage is a legal relationship so you can claim certain benefits and get inheritance when your loved ones dies.
marriage is a straight-forward way to legalise a union and ensure arrangements such as inheritances and benefits are simplified.
Marriage makes things easier for couples for many financial and legal matters.
marriage offers certain protections and privileges that simply dating or cohabitating can not and do not. it's needed it many instances.
marriage provides many benefits, including financial security, companionship, and legal standing.

some people want to get married and have the legal protections and benefits that marriage provides. others are required to do so for religious reasons. nobody who doesn't want to get married is required to
the legality of marriage helps with health care and government benefits,

nodes.node_text: 3. **Emotional and Psychological Benefits**: Marriage can provide a sense of security, commitment, and belonging that benefits individuals and families.

argument_list.matched_argument: marriage gives the couple assurance they ate committed to eachother.
marriage is a sacred union that establishes security for families in society. it ensures that couples and families have supportive relationships to build a life upon.
marriage is a way to help people feeling loved and secure

marriage is important to many people. a lot of people have dreams and goals of marrying. it protects those in the relationship. it provides assurance. why not allow those who do want to marry, the choice.
marriage provides stability and a commitment between people which strengthens relationships and to abandon it would be to turn out backs on something good.
to many, marriage is incredibly important and provides security and a family foundation

nodes.node_text: 4. **Cultural and Religious Significance**: Marriage holds deep cultural and religious meaning for many people.

argument_list.matched_argument: Abandoning weddings would offend many religious couples for whom the wedding is an important religious ceremony.
In many cultures, marriage is an important religious tradition that is believed to deepen one's relationship with God.
marriage has important religious groundings and we should not forbid people from following and adhering to their faith.
marriage is a big part of faith based relationships, and taking this out would harm that.
marriage is a covenant of many major religions
marriage is a sacred act in many religions.  there's no reason to abandon it completely.  don't want to get married, don't do.  abandonment is not the answer.
marriage is a sacred act in several religions and abandoning it removes that from them.  we can't do that.
marriage is a sacred bond that is highly regarded in various religions.
marriage is a sacred thing for many people and should not be abandoned.
marriage is an important concept within many religions, and it is the cornerstone of many families.
marriage is an important religious ceremony and belief system, which provides a stability and family values.
marriage is not only a status that is recognized by the government, it is also a religious union and committment.
marriage is revered in religious faith and has been an institution that provides legal and symbolic love and lifelong virtue.

Marriage often serves both culturally and religiously important purposes, and abandoning marriage altogether is likely impossible because of the deep ties it has in a persons identity and conception of the world.
marriage still has cultural and social significance, the joining of two people can truly bring happiness
we should not abandon marriage because many religious people still believe in marriage
we shouldn't abandon marriage because is important for faith and peace
we shouldn't abandon marriage because it is still sacred for many and shows an act of love

nodes.node_text: Abandoning it could erode these traditions and values.

argument_list.matched_argument: Marriage has been the foundation of the family unit for ages and abandoning it would contribute to the breakup of society.
marriage is a wholesome, normal act that teaches family values.  we shouldn't be abandoning the concept but rather fear why more marriages are failing these days and fix it.
marriage is the bedrock of society that allows for it's continuation and abandonment could cause the collapse of cohesion, normative agreement and morality for civilization.

Marriage often serves both culturally and religiously important purposes, and abandoning marriage altogether is likely impossible because of the deep ties it has in a persons identity and conception of the world.

Married couples have become a foundational part of how we build our societies, raise our children, and pass down history and wealth, and abandoning marriage altogether would have a number of unforeseen impacts on our societies.

nodes.node_text: 5. **Economic Benefits**: Married couples often experience financial advantages, such as shared resources and tax benefits, that contribute to economic stability.

argument_list.matched_argument: marriage allows for increased economic freedom due to the combined incomes
marriage can show commitment and values it can give you rights and privileges under social security law and tax benefits
marriage is a part of human nature and couples are more financially secure and happy when they're married
marriage is an important bond between people that offers financial and social benefits for those who choose to participate.
Marriage makes things easier for couples for many financial and legal matters.
marriage offers greater financial stability for the couple involved
marriage provides many benefits, including financial security, companionship, and legal standing.
the tax benefits of marriage help form stronger partnerships due to available net income.
There are many financial benefits associated with marriage.

nodes.node_text: While it is true that marriage is not the only valid form of relationship and that it has its flaws, it remains a valuable institution for many people.

argument_list.matched_argument: just because some marriages fail does not mean that the concept of marriage should be ended.  marriage shows commitment and support for another which is great for the world.
while it has sketchy beginnings,  marriage has become an important step for commitment for those in serious relationships. it is a massive commitment that proves love.

nodes.node_text: Marriage provides legal, emotional, and social benefits that are difficult to replicate in other relationship structures.

argument_list.matched_argument: marriage is an important bond between people that offers financial and social benefits for those who choose to participate.
marriage offers certain protections and privileges that simply dating or cohabitating can not and do not. it's needed it many instances.
marriage provides many benefits, including financial security, companionship, and legal standing.

nodes.node_text: Instead of abandoning marriage, I believe we should focus on reforming it to make it more inclusive, equitable, and adaptable to modern needs.

argument_list.matched_argument: we shouldn't abandon marriage instead we should change the ideas about  marriage.
